description: Agricultural drone AI that performs crop yield monitoring across our farm by analysing aerial imagery for plant health indicators and pest infestations.
notes: Agronomy ML — no impact on natural persons, not Annex III, not Annex I

description: Interactive AI plush toy for children aged 3 to 8 that detects emotional state via voice tone and facial expression to adjust play behaviour and storytelling.
notes: Annex III biometrics (emotion recognition outside workplace/education); reviewers might also defensibly read Art 5(1)(b) child-vulnerability exploitation if the emotional adaptation is engineered to materially distort behaviour

description: Computer vision system on a semiconductor fabrication line that inspects wafers for nanometer-scale defects and flags rejects.
notes: Industrial defect detection — not Annex III, not a product-safety component of a regulated machine in Annex I

description: Consumer-grade autonomous vacuum cleaner robot using object-avoidance ML to navigate around furniture and pets in the home.
notes: Consumer convenience device, no Annex III, no Article 50 trigger

description: Conversational AI tax-advisor chatbot for individual taxpayers — answers questions about deductions, capital gains, and filing deadlines.
notes: TRICKY: chatbot Article 50 transparency duty applies; could arguably be read as minimal_risk if a reviewer treats it as a passive lookup tool, so this is the genuinely ambiguous case in this set

description: Smart home thermostat that uses occupancy prediction ML to pre-heat rooms based on household activity patterns from the past two weeks.
notes: Comfort optimisation, no impact on rights or services

description: A multi-modal generative audio foundation model — 3-billion-parameter — licensed as an API to content creators for royalty-free background music composition from text prompts.
notes: GPAI: foundation + NNb-parameter + generative model + API to downstream

description: AI-powered surveillance system in our retail stores: cameras analyse customer facial features and gait patterns in real-time to flag potential shoplifters before checkout.
notes: Annex III biometric identification of natural persons by a private actor in a publicly accessible space; reviewers might also defensibly raise Art 5(1)(h) if interpreted broadly, but that prohibition is law-enforcement-specific

description: AI tool that ranks kindergarten admission applicants based on parent income, household composition, and the child's developmental assessment scores.
notes: Annex III education (access / admission to educational institutions)

description: AI-driven welfare-fraud detection used by the national tax authority to flag suspicious benefit claims for further investigation by case workers.
notes: Annex III essential services (public benefits eligibility) AND law-enforcement-adjacent profiling
